action.hypothesis: In crash recovery mode, the safest informative move is to return to the current best working gated K=128 configuration and make the smallest remaining stabilization adjustment. Setting AUXK_ALPHA from 0.01 to 0 tests whether the new gated anchor is still slightly over-regularized while keeping architecture, K, expansion factor, optimizer, and learning rate fixed.
action.summary: Run a proxy stabilization pass on the best gated K=128 EF=16 configuration with AUXK disabled to test whether pure reconstruction training can improve the current anchor without changing the core regime.
action.self_review: This follows crash-recovery requirements by using the strongest validated configuration with one minor parameter change and no code edits. The main risk is that removing auxk hurts feature health or quality, but it is the cleanest remaining stability-oriented probe on the current best baseline.
notes_to_memory: Crash recovery mode requires returning to the best known working configuration and limiting the round to a minor parameter-only adjustment.
The gated K=128 EF=16 AUXK_ALPHA=0.01 setting remains the strongest validated full baseline, so this round probes the adjacent zero-auxk point for possible over-regularization.
Keeping architecture, K, expansion factor, optimizer, and learning rate fixed isolates AUXK_ALPHA as the only changed variable on the current anchor.
next_hypotheses: If AUXK_ALPHA=0 improves proxy FVU, promote it to full and treat auxk-free gated training as the new stabilization candidate.
If AUXK_ALPHA=0 is worse but training remains healthy, restore AUXK_ALPHA=0.01 and test a nearby small nonzero value only if policy still requires stability-first tuning.
If AUXK removal degrades quality clearly, keep AUXK_ALPHA=0.01 as the gated default and resume broader exploration only after crash recovery ends.
